criteria.name: rate_limiter_mechanism
criteria.description: Explains that RateLimiter uses a concurrency limit with a Promise-based queue and optional timeout via Promise.race.
criteria.groundTruth: RateLimiter maintains activeCount and a queue of resolvers; waitForSlot blocks until a slot is available; withTimeout races the operation against a setTimeout reject.

criteria.name: signing_mechanism
criteria.description: Explains that Signer uses HMAC-SHA256 to create a signed token containing the value, a timestamp, and the signature.
criteria.groundTruth: Signer.sign() builds payload = value + '.' + timestamp, then computes HMAC-SHA256 signature and appends it. unsign() recomputes and verifies with hmac.compare_digest.

criteria.name: comparison_quality
criteria.description: Provides a meaningful comparison between the two approaches — concurrency control vs. authenticity verification, queue-based vs. cryptographic.
criteria.groundTruth: RateLimiter controls execution flow via queue + concurrency count; Signer ensures data integrity via HMAC. RateLimiter uses Promise.race for timeout; Signer uses hmac.compare_digest for timing-safe comparison.
